# Patti Smith Real Estate, Placerville, California

> El Dorado County real estate broker serving Placerville, the county seat, and the surrounding foothills. Specialist in historic downtown homes, in-town and rural property, wells and septic, fire hazard zones, county planning and permitting, and Gold Country real estate.

## About
- Broker: Patti Smith (Patti Smith Real Estate, Independent)
- License: CA DRE #01110483
- Office: 6180 State Highway 193, Georgetown, CA 95634
- Phone: (530) 368-0852
- Serving the Sierra Nevada foothills since 1992
- Board member, Marshall Medical Center (Finance and Marketing committees); past president of the Georgetown Rotary Club

## Placerville, CA at a glance
- County seat of El Dorado County and an incorporated city since May 13, 1854; county seat since 1857
- Originally Dry Diggings, then Hangtown, renamed Placerville for placer mining; population about 10,747 (2020); elevation about 1,867 feet; ZIP 95667; part of the Sacramento metropolitan area
- The canonical above-the-fog, below-the-snow foothill elevation; warm-summer Mediterranean climate
- Preserved 1850s Main Street on the National Register; Hangtown Creek runs through downtown; the city-owned Gold Bug Park and Mine; a former Pony Express route stop
- Two markets: in-town homes on city water and sewer, and surrounding rural acreage on wells and septic
- Schools: a patchwork of small districts, including Placerville Union (elementary and middle), El Dorado Union High School District (El Dorado High School), and surrounding K-8 districts such as Gold Trail and Gold Oak
- Home of Marshall Medical Center and El Dorado County government; on the Highway 50 corridor to Sacramento and Lake Tahoe
- Typical market: city median roughly $447,000 to $559,000; entry-level from the low-to-mid $300,000s; core market mid-$400,000s to mid-$600,000s
